observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Accountant to expedite the retrieval of the information about this account, secure the necessary supporting documents needed as required by COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, and submit a request for authority to write off from COA, in order to remove the long-outstanding and dormant accounts from the books of the agency.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the LDRRM Officer strictly follow the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the reporting and accounting of LDRRM Funds.

We recommended that management implement more strict controls in the payment of wages and salaries by directing the Disbursing Officer thru the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that payments should only be made directly to the names appearing in the payroll, otherwise, a written authority to collect should be presented by the person claiming the payment.

We recommended that the management consider adopting the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) payroll system, for greater transparency and efficiency and in order to safeguard government funds from the risk of loss or misapplication by disbursing officers. This will also significantly reduce operating costs in the handling and payment of cash to individual payees. It may also as one of the internal control measures that LGU’s may adopt to promote good governance at the local level.

We recommended the management to prepare and submit to the office of the auditor the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumptions for review as required by COA Circular No. 77-61 which provides the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles.
We commended the management on its effort to support priority programs and projects of the Department of Education with a recommendation to fully utilize the SEF to maximize the benefits thereof.

We commended the management for its effort in implementing solid waste management programs and continue encouraging the participation of every constituent in doing its share in environmental protection and maintenance.

1.5 We recommended the management to (1) require the liquidation of the outstanding cash advance balances by the concerned officials and employees and if no action was taken by them, consider to withhold their salaries, if warranted; (2) no additional cash advances shall be granted to officials and employees with unliquidated balances (3) strictly comply with the provisions of the abovementioned circular to avoid the penalty stated thereon.

2.4 We reiterated our audit recommendation that management conduct actual inventory of all the inventories remaining as at year-end  and reconcile with the accounting records to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.

3.5 We recommended the Municipal Accountant to expedite the retrieval of the information about this account, secure the necessary supporting documents needed as required by COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, and submit a request for authority to write off from COA, in order to remove the long-outstanding and dormant accounts from the books of the agency.

4.5 We recommended that the management reconcile their records with the Audit Team. We also recommend that the management exert all available remedies in enforcing the immediate settlement of all audit disallowances pursuant to pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-006 and to be more prudent in the processing of claims for payments to avoid audit disallowances and suspensions.

5.4 We recommended the following to the Management:
a. The Municipal Engineer shall provide the Municipal Accountant with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
b.	The LGU shall maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and shall keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.
c.	The Municipal Accountant shall prepare the Journal Voucher to record the inventory of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card.  He shall keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network, and ensure that 100% of the value of road network of the LGU shall be booked-up in 2019 consistent with COA Circular No. 2015-008. Full disclosure on road networks should be made in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the LGU.

6.4 We reiterated the previous years’ audit recommendations that management conduct actual inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and the corresponding report be prepared and submitted to the Office of the Auditor.  Also, the Accounting Office should prepare and maintain individual property ledger cards to comply with COA Circular No. 92-386. The actual inventory report prepared by the GSO/Inventory Committee should reconcile with accounting records and necessary adjustments should be made to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.

7.4 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the reciprocal accounts and adjust the balances in the financial statements. We also recommend that the Municipal Accountant review the reciprocal accounts on a regular basis to ensure the accuracy and reliability of the accounts.

8.5 We commended the management in the implementation of the listed PPAs in the AIP of the municipality with a recommendation that management ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% DF in accordance with Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 of the DILG and DBM to serve the intended purpose or use thereof to the constituents.
9.3 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct concerned officials to comply with the requirement in imposing liquidated damages as provided in Section 68 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
10.3 We recommended that management comply with the requirements of the above-stated regulation and ensure that all projects are implemented properly.

11.5 We recommended that management either compel the complete delivery of all the construction materials paid to the supplier or the return of the amount paid with an imposition of liquidated damages for the delay in the performance thereof.  Disbursements contrary to law will be disallowed in audit, if warranted.

12.8 We recommended that management refrain from charging expenditures that are not related to the implementation of DRRM activities. The total amount disbursed should be returned by the General Fund to avoid the depletion of the LDRRM Fund. Strict compliance to the provisions of the JMC 2013-1of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG, Section 21 of RA 10121 is highly recommended.
We also recommended the management to provide justification of the additional observations stated above. Unjustified expenses may be disallowed in audit, if warranted.

13.4 We recommended management to ensure that all supporting documents are properly accomplished and corresponding regulations for each type of procurement are adhered to. Disbursements made contrary to rules and regulations will be disallowed in audit, if warranted.
14.4 We recommended the Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2015-009 to ensure the fair presentation of the financial statements of the agency.
15.15 We recommended the management to comply with the disaster minimum standards and strictly follow the provisions of RA 10121 to ensure efficient and effective respond to disasters.

16.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Liquidating Officer to deposit all collections as required by rules and regulation to prevent possible misappropriation, theft and loss of funds. Impose administrative sanctions if warranted.

17.5 We reiterated our recommendation that management furnish copies of infrastructure contracts and POs and their supporting documents to the audit team within five (5) working days from the execution of the contract as prescribed in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to enable the Auditor to conduct timely auditorial review and evaluation of contracts.

18.5 We recommended the management to submit to the Office of the Auditor the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumptions for CY 2021 for review as required by COA Circular No. 77-61.

19.3 We recommended that management provide the necessary supporting documents of the transactions to avoid suspension in audit and strictly adhere to the provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

20.4 We recommended that management implement more strict controls in the payment of salaries and wages by directing the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that payments should only be made directly to the names appearing in the payroll, otherwise, a written authority to collect should be presented by the person claiming the payment.

21.9 We recommended the management provide the necessary supporting documents of the transactions to avoid suspension in audit and strictly adhere to the provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
22.7 We recommended management to strictly comply with the provisions of to Section 116 and 117 Volume 1 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGU).

23.5 We recommended that management submit immediately the duly accomplished AAPSI form to the audit team. We also recommend management to religiously submit AAPSI within sixty (60) days from receipt of COA Annual Audit Report.

24.7 We recommended that management require the Accountable Officers to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms as basis in the preparation of Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms pursuant to Sections 50 and 51 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume 2 and to ensure that all accountable forms received and issued are properly accounted for. The prompt submission of said monthly report to the Office of the Auditor constitutes timely audit review and verification.

25.5 We recommended management to comply with Section 53.5 of RA 9184 and its IRR, Section 4 of Administrative Order No. 17 and paragraph 1.1 of DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 in the procurement of Common-Use Supplies from PS-DBM.

26.8 We recommended management to procure common-use supplies from the Procurement Services, post all procurement opportunities in the PhilGEPS bulletin board, stop the practice of procuring supplies by piecemeal and adopt public bidding as the primary mode of procurement in compliance with RA No. 9184.

27.3 We reiterated our recommendation to management to issue policy guidelines in the hiring of job order/contractual employees in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 and Sections 209 and 211(b), Volume I of the GAAM and to strictly implement the same.

We recommended that management should always comply with laws and regulations covering the receipt and utilization of funds coming from National Agencies to ensure easier reporting and enhance transparency and accountability in the implementation of the intended programs, projects and activities.

We recommended that the LGU strictly adhere to the posting and reporting requirements as provided under DBM Local Budget Circular in the Project Implementation of the BGCM for transparency and accountability on the utilization thereof.

We recommended the management to stop the practice of reimbursements and granting of cash advances in procurement of goods. Resort to negotiated procurement adhering with the COA and GPPB Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated March 26, 2020 during the State of National Public Health Emergency.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant should prepare all the bank reconciliation and journal entry vouchers to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustments and correction in the General Ledger to present a reliable balance for the cash in bank account. Adherence to the provision of COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 74 of P.D. 1445 is required.

We reiterated the previous years’ audit recommendations that management conduct actual inventory of all properties owned by the LGU, the corresponding report be prepared and submitted to office of the auditor and prepare and maintain individual property ledger cards to comply with COA Circular No. 92-386. The inventory report prepared should reconcile with accounting records and necessary adjustments should be made to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.

We recommended the following to the Management:
a. The Municipal Engineer shall provide the OIC- Municipal Accountant with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
b. The LGU shall maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and shall keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.
c. The Municipal Accountant shall prepare the Journal Voucher to record the inventory of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card.  She shall keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network, and ensure that 100% of the value of road network of the LGU shall be booked-up in 2019 consistent with COA Circular No. 2015-008. Full disclosure on road networks should be made in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the LGU.
We recommended that management comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the reportorial requirements of the fund.

We reiterated our recommendation that management furnish the approved/perfected infrastructure contracts, POs and their supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five (5) working days from the execution of the contract as prescribed in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to enable the Audit Team to conduct a timely auditorial review and evaluation of contracts and communicate to the agency the results thereof.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (MA) ensure timely submission of accounts to facilitate the audit of the transactions as well as to avoid the possible suspension or disallowance of the same and the imposition of administrative disciplinary sanctions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Liquidating Officer to deposit all collections as required by rules and regulation to prevent possible misappropriation, theft and loss of funds. Impose administrative sanctions if warranted.

We recommended that management (a) establish clear-cut guidelines for the grant of telephone expenses to officials and employees; and (b) consider adopting the post-paid plans offered by the network providers for economic reasons and to foster equity in the grant of allowances in relation to actual communication needs of the LGU.

We recommended that management provide the necessary supporting documents of the transactions to avoid suspension in audit and strictly adhere to the provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended the management to (1) issue final demand letters to the concerned officials and employees and if no action was taken by them, consider to withhold from their salaries, if warranted; (2) no additional cash advances be granted to officials and employees with existing cash advances (3) strictly comply with the provisions of the abovementioned circular to avoid the penalty stated thereon.

We reiterated our recommendation that the management strictly enforce immediate settlement of audit disallowances to ensure recovery of municipal funds that were either illegally, irregularly or improperly disbursed and to be more prudent in the processing of claims for payments to avoid audit disallowances and suspensions. Also, we recommend the Municipal Accountant to review her record of disallowances and make an adjustment if necessary.

We recommended that management issue policy guidelines in the hiring of job order/contractual employees in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 and Sections 209 and 211(b), Volume I of the GAAM and to strictly implement the same.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Liquidating Officer to deposit all collections as required by rules and regulation to prevent possible misappropriation, theft and loss of funds. Impose administrative sanctions if warranted.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts of the municipality and record all valid reconciling items in the General Ledger to present a reliable balance for the cash in bank account.  Adherence to the provision of COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 74 of P.D. 1445 is required.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant should review the said account, secure the necessary supporting documents needed as required by COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, and submit a request for authority to write off from COA, in order to remove the long-outstanding and dormant accounts from the books of the agency.
We recommended that management should strictly comply with COA Circular No. 77-61 which provides the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure timely submission of accounts. Accordingly, he should cause the immediate submission of the unsubmitted accounts of the LGU for CY 2018 to facilitate the audit of the transactions as well as to avoid the possible suspension or disallowance of the same and the imposition of administrative disciplinary sanctions.

We reiterated our recommendation that management furnish the approved/perfected infrastructure contracts and their supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five (5) working days from the execution of the contract as prescribed in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to enable the Audit Team to conduct a timely auditorial review and evaluation of contracts and communicate to the agency the results thereof.

The inventory report prepared should reconcile with accounting records and necessary adjustments should be made to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.

observations.managementAction: The LGU has started its physical count of its property, plant and equipment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to comply with the provisions of PPSAS 12 relative to the recording of inventories.
observations.managementAction: For the Calendar Year (CY) 2020, the Municipal Accountant used asset method in recording procured supplies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to the Management:

The Municipal Engineer shall provide the Municipal Accountant with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

The LGU shall maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and shall keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.

The Municipal Accountant shall prepare the Journal Voucher to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card.  He shall keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network, and ensure that 100% of the value of road network of the Municipality shall be booked-up in 2019 consistent with COA Circular No. 2015-008. Full disclosure on road networks should be made in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineering Office has started preparing the road maps and road inventory report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the foregoing misstatements in the financial statements of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries were made and recorded by the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that cash advances which remained unliquidated beyond the allowed time of liquidation be withheld from the salaries of the accountable officers and proper charges be made, if warranted. Strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004 is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were issued by the Municipal Accountant to the Accountable Officers and some were already liquidated.

observations.recommendation: We commended the management for its effort in implementing solid waste management programs with a recommendation to continue encouraging the participation of every constituent in doing its share in environmental protection and maintenance.

We recommended that management settle the disallowances by withholding the payment of the salaries or any amount or cash due to the accountable/liable persons and request from COA for installment payment.
observations.managementAction: The appropriation for Gender and Development (GAD) related Programs, Projects and Activities (PPAs) was fully utilized in 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Accountable Officer to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms as basis in the preparation of Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms pursuant to Sections 50 and 51 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume 2 and to ensure that all accountable forms received and issued are properly accounted for. The prompt submission of said monthly report to the Office of the Auditor constitutes timely audit review and verification.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer prepared and submitted MRAAF in 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

Management particularly the HRMO and Accounting Office strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations stated above in the preparation and approval of the payrolls to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance thereof. 

In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than P5,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth. The other deductions that are not accommodated during the pay period should be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institution. 
The HRMO exercise diligence in certifying the monthly net take home pay of employees and provide emergency measures to assuage the situation of those who have been negatively affected by their loan deductions.
observations.managementAction: Employees were encouraged to pay their loans from the private financial institutions  personally and directly.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management provide the necessary supporting documents of the transactions to avoid suspension in audit and strictly adhere to the provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that management stop the payment of fixed travelling expenses in lieu of the transportation allowance of the elective officials and head of offices. Compliance to the above-cited regulations is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: The LGU stopped the payment of fixed travelling expenses to the officers and employees. All expenses incurred for travelling were properly supported in 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Liquidating Officer to deposit all collections as required by rules and regulation to prevent possible misappropriation, theft and loss of funds. Impose administrative sanctions if warranted.

We recommended that management provide the necessary supporting documents of the transactions to avoid suspension in audit and strictly adhere to the provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that management implement more strict controls in the payment of wages and salaries by directing the Disbursing Officer thru the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that payments should only be made directly to the names appearing in the payroll, otherwise, a written authority to collect should be presented by the person claiming the payment.

We recommended that management stop the payment of fixed travelling expenses in lieu of the transportation allowance of the elective officials and head of offices. Compliance to the above-cited regulations is enjoined.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts of the municipality and record all valid reconciling items in the General Ledger to present a reliable balance for the cash in bank account.  Adherence to the provision of COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 74 of P.D. 1445 is required.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the Bank Reconciliation Statements however not all reconciling items were recorded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the LDRRM Officer strictly follow the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.  2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the reporting and accounting of LDRRM Funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has recorded the unutilized LDRRM Fund as a Special Trust Fund under account “Trust Liability DRRM” in the Trust Fund Books as of December 31, 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the foregoing misstatements in the financial statements of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: Some entries were already corrected by the Municipal Accountant but there are still various transactions that are erroneously recorded as Construction In Progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that cash advances which remained unliquidated beyond the allowed time of liquidation be withheld from the salaries of the accountable officers and proper charges be made, if warranted. Strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004 is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: Some were already settled and liquidated as of December 31, 2019. Cash advances to officers and employees decreased from ₱22,491,240.18 to ₱13,747,797.04 in 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should strictly comply with COA Circular No. 77-61 which provides the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles and Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445. All expenses regarding any unauthorized use or misuse of government motor transportation shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Driver’s trip tickets attached to vouchers are properly filled out and with serial numbers in 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly monitor and fast track the implementation and completion of all the LGU’s programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund.
observations.managementAction: Some were already implemented in 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the reportorial requirements.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Office regularly submits Report on Sources and Utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund to the office of the Auditor in 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management should comply with the provisions of RA 10121 on the creation of plantilla positions for LDRRM Officer and Assistants.

We commended the management for its effort in implementing solid waste management programs with a recommendation to continue encouraging the participation of every constituent in doing its share in environmental protection and maintenance.
We commended the management in implementing various PPAs which are supportive of GAD and also recommended that the implementation of PPAs should be maximized to attain the purpose of GAD.
observations.managementAction: The appropriation for Gender and Development (GAD) related Programs, Projects and Activities (PPAs) was fully utilized in 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management settle the disallowances by withholding the payment of the salaries or any amount or cash due to the accountable/liable persons and request from COA for installment payment.

We commended the management for its efforts in implementing programs and projects that protect children’s rights in the municipality with a recommendation that the implementation of programs and projects should be maximized.
observations.managementAction: Appropriations for priority programs, projects and activities included in the  Annual Investment Program for Children was fully utilized in 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure timely submission of accounts. Accordingly, he should cause the immediate submission of the unsubmitted accounts of the LGU for CY 2018 to facilitate the audit of the transactions as well as to avoid the possible suspension or disallowance of the same and the imposition of administrative disciplinary sanctions.
observations.managementAction: Some are still submitted beyond the deadline.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management furnish the approved/perfected infrastructure contracts and their supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five (5) working days from the execution of the contract as prescribed in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to enable the Audit Team to conduct a timely auditorial review and evaluation of contracts and communicate to the agency the results thereof.
observations.managementAction: Some are still submitted beyond the deadline.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

Management particularly the HRMO and Accounting Office strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations stated above in the preparation and approval of the payrolls to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance thereof.

In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than P5,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth. The other deductions that are not accommodated during the pay period should be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institution. 
The HRMO exercise diligence in certifying the monthly net take home pay of employees and provide emergency measures to assuage the situation of those who have been negatively affected by their loan deductions.
observations.managementAction: Some of the employees found with net take home pay below the required amount in 2018 already complied in 2019, however there are still some that are receiving net pay below the required amount.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts of the municipality and record all valid reconciling items in the General Ledger to present a reliable balance for the cash in bank account.  Adherence to the provision of COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 74 of P.D. 1445 is required.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office is still in process in retrieving documents to support the reconciling items. As of date of the audit, the Municipal Accountant failed to provide the Bank Reconciliation Statement for the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should issue final demand letters to all active officials and employees with outstanding balances and suspend their salaries, if warranted.  Strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004 is enjoined.
observations.managementAction: None. The unliquidated cash advances increased from ₱19,166,476.91 in 2017 to ₱22,491,240.18 in 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant prepare and submit a request for authority to write off from COA together with the necessary supporting documents needed as required by COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 to remove the long-outstanding and dormant accounts from the books of the agency.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office is still in the process of identifying the details and retrieving supporting documents of this account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management conduct actual inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and the corresponding report be prepared and submitted to comply with COA Circular No. 92-386.  The inventory report prepared by the GSO should reconcile with accounting records and necessary adjustments should be made to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Acting LDRRM Officer strictly follow the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.  2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the reporting and accounting of LDRRM Funds.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to comply with the provisions of PPSAS 12 relative to the recording of inventories.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the foregoing misstatements in the financial statements of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to strictly monitor and ensure the implementation and completion of all LGU PPAs to immediately serve the intended purpose or use thereof to the constituents.  Likewise, the management should ensure compliance to the guidelines set under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 for the optimal utilization of the fund.
observations.managementAction: Several government programs, projects and activities (PPAs) with an aggregate cost of ₱6,769,000.00 from the current and continuing appropriation were not undertaken/implemented in 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the reportorial requirements.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the concerned DepEd employees to liquidate their cash advances immediately and ensure that only those expenditures which are supportive of the operation and maintenance of public schools and learning centers will be charged in the Special Education Fund.  No cash advance shall be granted unless the outstanding balances is liquidated by them. Disbursements contrary to rules and regulations will be disallowed in audit, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.

observations.recommendation: We commended the management in implementing various PPAs which are supportive of GAD. We also recommend that the implementation of PPAs should be maximized to attain the purpose of GAD.
observations.managementAction: Only 40% was utilized in 2018.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management furnish the approved/perfected infrastructure contracts and their supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five (5) working days from the execution of the contract as prescribed in COA Circular 2009 001 dated February 12, 2009 to enable the Audit Team to conduct a timely auditorial review and evaluation of contracts and communicate to the agency the results thereof.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

Management particularly the HRMO and Accounting Office strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations stated above in the preparation and approval of the payrolls to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance thereof. 

In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than ₱4,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth.  The other deductions that are not accommodated during the pay period should already be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institution. 

The HRMO exercise diligence in certifying the monthly net take home pay of employees and provide emergency measures to assuage the situation of those who have been negatively affected by their loan deductions.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management issue policy guidelines in the hiring of job order/contractual employees in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 and Sections 209 and 211(b), Volume I of the GAAM and to strictly implement the same.
observations.managementAction: No action taken.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Liquidating Officer to deposit all collections as required by rules and regulation to prevent possible misappropriation, theft and loss of funds. Impose administrative sanctions if warranted.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts of the municipality and record all valid reconciling items in the General Ledger to present a reliable balance for the cash in bank account.  Adherence to the provision of COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 74 of P.D. 1445 is required.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant should review the said account, secure the necessary supporting documents needed as required by COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, and submit a request for authority to write off from COA, in order to remove the long-outstanding and dormant accounts from the books of the agency.

We reiterated our recommendation that management should conduct actual inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and the corresponding report be prepared and submitted to comply with COA Circular No. 92-386.  The inventory report prepared by the Municipal Treasurer should reconcile with accounting records and necessary adjustments should be made to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the LDRRM Officer strictly follow the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.  2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the reporting and accounting of LDRRM Funds.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to comply with the provisions of PPSAS 12 relative to the recording of inventories.

We recommended the following to the Management:
The Municipal Engineer shall provide the Municipal Accountant with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
The LGU shall maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and shall keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.
The Municipal Accountant shall prepare the Journal Voucher to record the inventory of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card.  He shall keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network, and ensure that 100% of the value of road network of the Municipality shall be booked-up in 2019 consistent with COA Circular No. 2015-008. Full disclosure on road networks should be made in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the LGU.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the foregoing misstatements in the financial statements of the municipality.

We recommended that cash advances which remained unliquidated beyond the allowed time of liquidation be withheld from the salaries of the accountable officers and proper charges be made, if warranted. Strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004 is enjoined.

We recommended that management should strictly comply with COA Circular No. 77-61 which provides the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles and Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445. All expenses regarding any unauthorized use or misuse of government motor transportation shall be disallowed in audit.
We recommended that management should strictly comply with COA Circular No. 77-61 which provides the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles.
We recommended that Management strictly monitor and fast track the implementation and completion of all the LGU’s programs, projects and activities funded under the 20% Development Fund.
We recommended that management comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the reportorial requirements.
We recommended that Management should comply with the provisions of RA 10121 on the creation of plantilla positions for LDRRM Officer and Assistants.

We commended the management for its effort in implementing solid waste management programs with a recommendation to continue encouraging the participation of every constituent in doing its share in environmental protection and maintenance.
We commended the management in implementing various PPAs which are supportive of GAD and also recommended that the implementation of PPAs should be maximized to attain the purpose of GAD.

We recommended that management settle the disallowances by withholding the payment of the salaries or any amount or cash due to the accountable/liable persons and request from COA for installment payment.

We commended the management for its efforts in implementing programs and projects that protect children’s rights in the municipality with a recommendation that the implementation of programs and projects should be maximized.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure timely submission of accounts. Accordingly, he should cause the immediate submission of the unsubmitted accounts of the LGU for CY 2018 to facilitate the audit of the transactions as well as to avoid the possible suspension or disallowance of the same and the imposition of administrative disciplinary sanctions.

We reiterated our recommendation that management furnish the approved/perfected infrastructure contracts and their supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five (5) working days from the execution of the contract as prescribed in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to enable the Audit Team to conduct a timely auditorial review and evaluation of contracts and communicate to the agency the results thereof.

We recommended that management require the Accountable Officer to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms as basis in the preparation of Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms pursuant to Sections 50 and 51 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System for LGUs, Volume 2 and to ensure that all accountable forms received and issued are properly accounted for. The prompt submission of said monthly report to the Office of the Auditor constitutes timely audit review and verification.

We recommended the following:
Management particularly the HRMO and Accounting Office strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations stated above in the preparation and approval of the payrolls to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance thereof.
In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than P5,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth. The other deductions that are not accommodated during the pay period should be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institution.
The HRMO exercise diligence in certifying the monthly net take home pay of employees and provide emergency measures to assuage the situation of those who have been negatively affected by their loan deductions.

We recommended that management issue policy guidelines in the hiring of job order/contractual employees in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 and Sections 209 and 211(b), Volume I of the GAAM and to strictly implement the same.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the liquidating officer deposit all collections in order to prevent misappropriation, theft and loss of funds. Impose administrative sanctions if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Undeposited collections decreased from ₱879,323.70 in 2017 to ₱717,641.32 in 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and General Services Officer endeavor to identify all completed and on-going projects funded by other national government agencies for the proper recognition in the books of accounts of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Recorded in the books of the LGU in January 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should make a careful perusal of the accounts payables with the view of reverting the same to the Government Equity Account if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Adjusted in 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management evaluate the status of project implementation and identify the reason for the delay in the completion of the projects funded by NGAs. Violation of contractors in the implementation of projects shall be a ground for the termination of their contract and forfeiture of their performance bond in favor of the government.
observations.managementAction: All projects funded by the National Government Agencies (NGAs) were 100% completed in 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management should ensure that the 20% Development Fund of the LGU be allotted and used for priority development programs, projects and activities which shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.
observations.managementAction: The management has complied and enforced the rules and regulation on the implementation of  the 20% Development Fund (DF).

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management refrain from charging expenditures that are not related to the implementation of DRRM activities. The total amount disbursed should be returned by the General Fund to avoid the depletion of the LDRRM Fund. Also, overtime plan/program and the actual accomplishment report should be submitted to support the claim for overtime pay.
observations.managementAction: The Management has complied with the provisions of Section 21 of RA 10121 and Section 3 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 in 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for management to comply with the minimum standards on disaster preparedness specifically on service delivery to ensure that support is in place during emergencies.
observations.managementAction: In 2018, the LGU has maintained a Memorandum of Agreement with grocery stores for the deliveries of needed food incase of emergency and distribution.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management comply with the provisions of RA 10121 on the creation of plantilla positions for LDRRM Officer and Assistants.
observations.managementAction: The LGU hired one (1) LDRRM Assistant in February 2019.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer make strategies, conduct massive tax information campaign and exert efforts to ensure collection of all realty taxes. Moreover, demand letters should be sent regularly to the delinquent owners, and if warranted, their names including the amount of their realty tax delinquencies should be posted in public places so that the public may know their identities.
observations.managementAction: The LGU has implemented strategies and conducted massive tax information campaign. As a result, collection efficiency of the LGU has increased to 91.38% in 2018.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management comply strictly with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund without prejudice to the return of the amount to the fund so that it will be utilized for its intended purpose.
observations.managementAction: The loan was fully paid in 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to facilitate the settlement of disallowed payments, and for the Disbursing Officer/cashier to withhold from the salaries of accountable officers should they fail to settle their disallowances despite the issuance of notice and demand.
observations.managementAction: The disallowances decreased only by ₱21,300.00 in 2018.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management to consider the inclusion in the succeeding annual budget a provision for the immediate repair of the two tractors in order to generate income for payment of its amortization.  In consonance, the Local Sanggunian must enact tax ordinance of its operation.  In addition, the Municipal Agricultural Officer must consider the use of the mini warehouse for embankment of fertilizers/seeds coming from the Department of Agriculture as subsidy to farmers.
observations.managementAction: Management did not consider inclusion of repair of the farm equipment because the possible benefit will not exceed the cost.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the local Sangguniang Bayan amend, modify or repeal in accordance with section 191 of RA 7160 the local tax ordinances found to be obsolete, and to prescribe or fixed the rates to be followed in the Collection of Local taxes.  For this purpose amended or modified tax ordinances be published in full for three (3) consecutive days in a newspaper of local circulation, and in the absence of newspapers of local circulation, the same maybe posted at least in two (2) conspicuous and publicly accessible places (Section 188).
observations.managementAction: Management failed to formulate new tax ordinance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management consider adopting the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) payroll system, for greater transparency and efficiency and to safeguard government funds from the risk of loss or misapplication by disbursing officers.  This will also significantly reduce operating cost in the handling and payment of cash to individual payees.
observations.managementAction: Payments of salaries are still made over the counter through the cashier.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that officials and employees of the municipal government with outstanding cash advance be directed to immediately liquidate/settle their cash advances to avoid the possibility of being charged due to misuse of government funds.  If warranted, withhold the salaries of those who fail or refuse to comply with the regulations.  Henceforth, monitor closely all outstanding cash advances and demand immediately the liquidations.  Comply strictly with COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2014-004 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant issued demand letters. However, despite of this action taken by the Municipal Accountant, cash advances increased from P14,045,268.27 to  P17,589,976.91. This is due to granting of cash advances to employees with unsettled balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management evaluate the amount of bond coverage and whenever necessary, increase their coverage with the Fidelity Bond of the Bureau of Treasury especially the Municipal Treasurer since they are handling large sums of money turned over to them by the collecting officers pending deposits to the authorized depository bank.
observations.managementAction: Management maintained the fidelity bond paid base on previous years.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management furnish the approve/perfected infrastructure contracts and their supporting documents to the Audit Team for review with in five (5) working days from the execution of the contract as prescribed in COA Circular 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 to enable the Audit team to conduct a timely auditorial review and evaluation of contracts and communicate to the agency the results thereof.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Acting LDRRM Officer strictly follow the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the reporting and accounting of LDRRM Funds.
observations.managementAction: The required report was not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the unappropriated balance of the fund be included as beginning balance in the preparation of SEF budget to maximized the utilization of the fund for programs and activities provided under Joint DECS,DBM,DILG Circular No. 01-3 dated June 25, 2001 and section 272 of RA 7160.

We reiterate our recommendation that management expedite the titling of the seventeen (17) parcel of land in the name of the Municipal Government of Aglipay, copy furnished the COA through the Audit Team assigned in the LGU of all the TCTs.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management enforce settlement of all audit disallowances from the persons deemed liable, thru cash refund, to ensure recovery of the funds of the LGU that were disbursed irregularly.  We recommend also management for being a compliant with COA Order of Execution.
observations.managementAction: No settlement made for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management stop the practice of procuring regular office supplies by department/office, instead require the BAC Secretariat to consolidate all needed supplies of departments/offices of the municipality based on their individual Project Procurement Plan (PPMP) to the Annual Procurement Plan (APP). Based on the APP, the BAC should adopt bulk purchases through public bidding to obtain the price most advantageous to the municipality pursuant to RA 9184 and its Revised IRR to promote transparency, efficiency and economy in the procurement of the office supplies.
observations.managementAction: Some supplies were still procured per office.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management comply strictly with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund without prejudice to the return of the amount to the fund so that it will be utilized for its intended purpose.
observations.managementAction: Amortizations for the loan were still included on the 20% DF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the foregoing misstatements in the financial statements of the municipality.  Also, the grant of cash advance should be made directly to the Disbursing Officer or to Special Disbursing Officer pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002
observations.managementAction: Cash advances remained unliquidated at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the balances of reciprocal accounts so that only the net amount shall be reflected in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Management still in the process of looking for the reconciling items.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary documents need for the write - off as required by COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 and submit the request to COA for write-off of long outstanding receivables particularly those from retired or deceased employees.
observations.managementAction: None.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the liquidating officer deposit all collections in order to prevent misappropriation, theft and loss of funds. Impose administrative sanctions if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Bank Reconciliation Statements for all bank accounts of the municipality and record all valid reconciling items in the General Ledger to present a reliable balance for the cash in bank account.  Adherence to the provision of COA Circular No. 96-011 and Section 74 of P.D. 1445 is required.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should issue final demand letters to all active officials and employees with outstanding balances and suspend their salaries, if warranted.  Strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004 is enjoined.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant prepare and submit a request for authority to write off from COA together with the necessary supporting documents needed  as required by COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 to remove the long-outstanding and dormant accounts from the books of the agency.

We recommended that management conduct actual inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and the corresponding report be prepared and submitted to comply with COA Circular No. 92-386.  The inventory report prepared by the GSO should reconcile with accounting records and necessary adjustments should be made to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and General Services Officer endeavor to identify all completed and on-going projects funded by other national government agencies for the proper recognition in the books of accounts of the Municipality.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should make a careful perusal of the aforementioned accounts payables with the view of reverting the same to the Government Equity Account if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Acting LDRRM Officer strictly follow the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.  2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 on the reporting and accounting of LDRRM Funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the foregoing misstatements in the financial statements of the municipality.

We recommended that the management evaluate the status of project implementation and identify the reason for the delay in the completion of the projects funded by NGAs. Violation of contractors in the implementation of projects shall be a ground for the termination of their contract and forfeiture of their performance bond in favor of the government.

We recommended that management should ensure that the 20% Development Fund of the LGU be allotted and used for priority development programs, projects and activities which shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1.

We recommended the management to strictly monitor and ensure the implementation and completion of all LGU PPAs to immediately serve the intended purpose or use thereof to the constituents.  Likewise, the management should ensure compliance to the guidelines set under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 for the optimal utilization of the fund.

We recommended that management refrain from charging expenditures that are not related to the implementation of DRRM activities. The total amount disbursed should be returned by the General Fund to avoid the depletion of the LDRRM Fund. Also, overtime plan/program and the actual accomplishment report should be submitted to support the claim for overtime pay.
We recommended that management comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 on the reportorial requirements.
We recommended for management to comply with the minimum standards on disaster preparedness specifically on service delivery to ensure that support is in place during emergencies.
We recommended that management comply with the provisions of RA 10121 on the creation of plantilla positions for LDRRM Officer and Assistants.

No cash advance shall be granted unless the outstanding balances is liquidated by them. Disbursements contrary to rules and regulations will be disallowed in audit, if warranted.

We commended the management for its effort in implementing solid waste management programs with a recommendation to fast track the implementation of programmed solid waste programs and activities for the benefit of the constituents of the municipality.
We commended the management in implementing various PPAs which are supportive of GAD. We also recommend that the implementation of PPAs should be maximized to attain the purpose of GAD.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer make strategies, conduct massive tax information campaign and exert efforts to ensure collection of all realty taxes. Moreover, demand letters should be sent regularly to the delinquent owners, and if warranted, their names including the amount of their realty tax delinquencies should be posted in public places so that the public may know their identities.

We reiterated our recommendation that  management comply strictly with the provisions of DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 in the utilization of the 20% Development Fund without prejudice to the return of the amount to the fund so that it will be utilized for its intended purpose.

We recommended the management to facilitate the settlement of disallowed payments, and for the Disbursing Officer/cashier to withhold from the salaries of accountable officers should they fail to settle their disallowances despite the issuance of notice and demand.

We commended the management for its efforts in implementing programs and projects that protect children’s rights in the municipality with a recommendation that more programs which directly benefits and protects the children in the municipality will be implemented.

We reiterated our recommendation that management furnish the approved/perfected infrastructure contracts and their supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five (5) working days from the execution of the contract as prescribed in COA Circular 2009 001 dated February 12, 2009 to enable the Audit Team to conduct a timely auditorial review and evaluation of contracts and communicate to the agency the results thereof.

We recommended the following:
Management particularly the HRMO and Accounting Office strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations stated above in the preparation and approval of the payrolls to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance thereof.
In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than ₱4,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth.  The other deductions that are not accommodated during the pay period should already be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institution.
The HRMO exercise diligence in certifying the monthly net take home pay of employees and provide emergency measures to assuage the situation of those who have been negatively affected by their loan deductions.

We recommended that management issue policy guidelines in the hiring of job order/contractual employees in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 and Sections 209 and 211(b), Volume I of the GAAM and to strictly implement the same.

We recommended that the management to be more aggressive and efficient in the full implementation of development projects to ensure that the benefits derived from these shall be enjoyed by its constituents.  If the program/projects are determined to be "not implementable", the corresponding funds thereof shall be returned to the respective sources agencies. 

Return to the source agency the un-utilized balances of funds derived from fully completed projects, or to the general fund of the LGU, if the residual amounts pertain to local counterparts.
observations.managementAction: The Management was able to increase its utilization rate from 19.40% in 2016 to 66% in 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
1.   The Municipal Mayor, being the Chairman of the Municipal Development Council, impose sanctions to members of the MDC who will not attend meetings during the formulation of AIP.
2.   The Local Development Council and the Municipal Planning and Development Officer in coordination with the BIDs and Awards Committee formulate/develop specific strategies/solutions to effectively implement development projects.  Consider formulating a timely within which to complete the unimplemented projects.
3.   The Engineering Office improve the planning and scheduling of work activities and manpower in order to facilitate completion of program of work of unimplemented projects.  Ensure that plans are accurate and projects estimates are based on standard and realistic amount.
4.   The MPDO enhance monitoring the implementation of projects in coordination with the implementing offices and to submit regularly status reports of implementation to the Municipal Mayor for his information so that appropriate measures can be instituted to obtain optimal utilization of fund.
observations.managementAction: Annual Investment Plan for 2017 has been formulated

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management publish tax declaration numbers of the delinquent taxpayers so that they will be informed of their obligations. Also, direct the Municipal Treasurer to review the list of delinquent taxpayers. The ultimate goal of improving the collection efficiency and effectiveness ratio of RPT is not remote with the collective efforts of the legislative council by supporting the local revenue code, the office of the Municipal Assessor by undertaking timely revisions and simultaneous cleansing of the RPTAS database and the office of the Municipal Treasurer by strengthening the force of law in availing itself of other more effective and powerful remedies so provided in enforcing collection of delinquent accounts such administrative action thru levy on real property or by judicial action.
observations.managementAction: Management conducted massive tax campaign and visitation to delinquent taxpayers every quarter.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management strictly monitor and require the collectors to regularly remit intact all their collections to avoid misapplication of government funds and to reflect all income collected during the year.
observations.managementAction: Undeposited collections decreased from P1,023,991.41 in 2016 to P879,323.70 in 2017.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate our recommendation that management provide all infrastructure projects with tarpaulin signboard, adopting all the data and specifications required in annex A of COA Circular No. 2013-004, to inform the public of the essential feature/elements of the project to promote transparency and accountability.
observations.managementAction: Required signboards were installed on the various  infrastructure projects implemented for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management comply with the provisions of the IRR of RA 10121 through the provision in the annual budget salaries of a full pledge LDRRMO and one (1) LDRRM Assistant.
observations.managementAction: The management appointed an LDRRM Officer (SG-18) dated November 2, 2017 and was approved by the Civil Service Commission on November 8, 2017. However, the Annual Budget for CY 2017 showed that there was no budget provided for the required positions of the LDRRM Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant records in the books of the municipality the cost of the Public Infrastructure projects as well as cost of completed and constructed projects through trust funds.  In addition, the Municipal Accountant draw a journal entry voucher to transfer booked completed projects of P12,039,657.94 from the trust fund to the general fund books or to the concerned barangay.
observations.managementAction: The identification of completed projects in the books and matching of material costs and labor costs of completed projects are still on going, which the Municipal Accountant’s basis in recording the assets in its proper (PPE) account.

observations.recommendation: We again recommended that management: a) Formulate a GAD Plan with corresponding appropriation in the annual budget designed to address gender issues within the Municipality and its constituents; b) Budget at least five (5) percent of the municipality's total appropriation for the GAD; c) implement the GAD Plan, which shall be integrated in the regular activities of the LGU and prepare accomplishment reports for submission to agencies concerned; and d) GAD Focal person to prepare/control/record and monitor reports thereon.
observations.managementAction: A GAD Plan was prepared and projects thereto were implemented for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management direct the Chairman of the Bids and Awards Committee to procure the needed goods/supplies in bulk on a quarterly basis in accordance with the approved APP and as provided under the IRR of RA 9184, otherwise, piece-meal procurement in the future will be suspended/disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: Bidding process was observed for CY 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management make reconciliation with the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) relative to their outstanding loan balances in order the correct figure shall be reflected in the financial statement of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation was made.
Granting of transportation allowances to Municipal Officials with assigned vehicles was already stopped by the management in 2017.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the management to require the outgoing elective officials to secure clearance from money and property accountability and submit to the concerned office the properties issued to them.
observations.managementAction: The Management is in the process on retrieving the properties issued to out-going Elective Municipal Officials.
